xep.header:
Explicit Mentions This specification defines a way to explicitly mention a person or groups of people. &LEGALNOTICE; xxxx ProtoXEP Standards Track Standards Council
XMPP Core XEP-0001 XEP-0030 XEP-0045 XEP-0317 XEP-0421 XEP-0426
NOT_YET_ASSIGNED
mention
Snit Guckfung snit@cock.li snit@isekai.rocks
0.0.3 2026-03-13 snit
Revisions based on feedback
0.0.2 2026-02-25 snit
Proposal handover
0.0.1 2023-03-23 pep
First draft.

section1.p: A common feature in online communication is the ability to explicitly address a message to a specific person or a group of people, and have the recipient's client notify them. This document provides a mechanism to allow users to explicitly reference individual users, as well as entire groups based on roles, affiliations, and hats in their messages. Furthermore, it provides a way for &xep0045; room owners to specify permissions related to mentions.
An entity implementing this specification MUST advertise '&NS;' as a &xep0030; feature. Subsequent OPTIONAL features in this document MUST also advertise support, if implemented.
All mentions are in the form of an <mention /> element qualified with the '&NS;' namespace.

The 'type' attribute MUST be specified with a URI determining the type of mention (occupant ID, room, association, etc.). A mention MAY contain a 'target' attribute, which specifies who is being mentioned. The 'target' is only omitted if implied by the 'type'.

Both 'begin' and 'end' MUST be specified, corresponding to the range of body text containing the mention (i.e. the user's name, and possibly including extra characters like '@'). The range MUST be determined using &xep0426;.

example.caption: Mentioning a user in a room

section1.example: <message to='news@chat.commons.example' type='groupchat'> <body>Hello, Alice!</body> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' begin='7' end='12' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#occupant' target='dd72603deec90a38ba552f7c68cbcc61bca202cd'/> </message>
section1.p: The 'begin' and 'end' pairs SHOULD NOT overlap between multiple mentions, except in the case that they are all equal.

example.caption: Mentioning members of an informally-defined group in a channel with many occupants

section1.example: <message to='news@chat.commons.example' type='groupchat'> <body>Hi Spartacists!</body> <!-- The range corresponds to "Spartacists" for all mentions... --> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' begin='3' end='14' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#occupant' target='dd72603deec90a38ba552f7c68cbcc61bca202cd'/> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' begin='3' end='14' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#occupant' target='3deec90a38ba552f7c68cbcc61bca202cddd7260'/> </message>
section1.p: Any mention type MAY restrict the set of targetted users to only those who are currently active, akin to "@here" on legacy messengers, by setting 'active' to true. If an entity receives a mention that was sent when the user was not active, it SHOULD NOT notify them. It is up to implementations what constitutes "currently active". Examples include being online or recently interacting with the client.

example.caption: Mentioning all present users in a room

section1.example: <message to='news@chat.commons.example' type='groupchat'> <body>@here, I need to restart the server. We'll be back in a sec.</body> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' active='true' begin='0' end='5' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#channel'/> </message>

section2.p: Individual mentions are the minimum REQUIRED to support this specification, advertised via the '&NS;' namespace.
There are two ways to mention an individual, depending on context. In both cases, the 'target' attribute is REQUIRED.
In &xep0045; rooms which support &xep0421; identifiers, the 'type' is '&NS;#occupant' and the 'target' is the user's occupant ID.

example.caption: Mentioning a user in a room

section2.example: <message to='news@chat.commons.example' type='groupchat'> <body>Hello, Alice!</body> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' begin='7' end='12' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#occupant' target='dd72603deec90a38ba552f7c68cbcc61bca202cd'/> </message>
section2.p: In all other cases, the 'type' attribute is '&NS;#jid' and the 'target' is the user's JID. JID mentions MUST NOT be used if the context provides an occupant ID, even if the JID is already known.

example.caption: Referring to a different user in a 1:1 chat

section2.example: <message from='juliet@example.com' to='romeo@example.net' type='chat'> <body>You'll want to discuss this with nurse</body> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' begin='33' end='38' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#jid' target='nurse@example.com'/> </message>

section2.p: Users might wish to mention whole groups of users at once. The following subsections specify several types of groups that can be mentioned, all of which are OPTIONAL.

Each group type has its own URI for the 'type' attribute, which MUST also be advertised via &xep0030; if supported. Custom group types not specified in this document MAY be used, provided they follow the same rules.

section3.p: It is often useful to mention all occupants of the current room at once, similarly to "@everyone" or "@room" on legacy messengers. The 'type' for this mention is '&NS;#channel', which MUST be advertised if this feature is supported. As the target is all room occupants, the 'target' attribute is redundant, and MUST NOT be specified.

example.caption: Mention all users in a room

section3.example: <message to='news@chat.commons.example' type='groupchat'> <body>Hey, @room!</body> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#channel' begin='5' end='10'/> </message>

section3.p: It is possible to mention occupants with specific &xep0045; roles or affiliations. The 'type' for this feature is '&NS;#associations'. A role or affiliation mention MUST contain a 'target'. All of the values for 'target' below MUST be defined for this feature.

example.caption: Mention all moderators in a room

section3.example: <message to='news@chat.commons.example' type='groupchat'> <body>Any moderator to handle the spam above please?</body> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' begin='4' end='13' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#associations' target='moderators'/> </message>

section3.p: Hats, specified in &xep0317;, can also be mentioned using the '&NS;#hats' namespace. The 'target' attribute MUST contain the hat's URI.

example.caption: Mentioning all (hats) students in a room

section3.example: <message to='news@chat.commons.example' type='groupchat'> <body>To all students, [..]</body> <mention xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' begin='7' end='15' type='urn:xmpp:mentions:0#hats' target='http://schemas.example.com/hats#students'/> </message>

section2.p: A &xep0045; service MAY provide a permission form in 'muc#roomconfig' to allow room owners to filter, prohibit, or limit various uses of mentions. Support for the form MUST be advertised with '&NS;'.
At minimum, the 'mentions#count' and 'mentions#individual' fields MUST be present. All other fields are OPTIONAL, and MUST be present if the corresponding feature is advertised.

Mentions MAY be sent in rooms which do not have permissions set, and/or do not advertise support for them. Implementations MAY reference the form to determine whether the user should be allowed to send a mention type to begin with.
section2.example:
<iq from='room@chat.commons.example' to='user@chat.commons.example/client' id='roomconfig' type='result'> <query xmlns='http://jabber.org/protocol/muc#owner'> <x xmlns='jabber:x:data' type='form'> <field type='hidden' var='FORM_TYPE'> <value>http://jabber.org/protocol/muc#roomconfig</value> </field>
<!-- Standard room configuration fields... -->
<field var='mentions#count' label='How many mentions are allowed in a message?' type='text-single'> <value>5</value> </field>
<field var='mentions#individual' label='Who can mention individual users?' type='list-single'> <value>participants</value> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field>
<!-- If room supports urn:xmpp:mentions:0#channel: --> <field var='mentions#channel' label='Who can mention room occupants?' type='list-single'> <value>participants</value> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field>
<!-- If room supports urn:xmpp:mentions:0#associations: --> <field var='mentions#associations' label='Who can mention roles/affiliations?' type='list-single'> <value>participants</value> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field>
<!-- If room supports urn:xmpp:mentions:0#hats: --> <field var='mentions#hats' label='Who can mention users with hats?' type='list-single'> <value>participants</value> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field> </x> </query> </iq>

section1.p: This specification allows sending clients to convey a user's intent. Implementations SHOULD also allow sending participants to talk about someone without having to mention them.
Extra characters which are not part of the user's name, such as '@', could interfere with implicit mentions in some implementations.
Receiving entities always have final say on whether they think a mention refers to or notifies the user, and MAY specify further rules than specified here.

Most clients currently use regex-based solutions to determine whether a user is to be notified of a message. This is prone to false positives, especially for users with common words as names. It also provides no mechanism for a user to refer to someone else without notifying them.

&xep0224; could be used to provide similar functionality to an active room mention. However, it recommends only being used among contacts, cannot specify a range of associated body text, and cannot notify inactive users.
&xep0372; provides a basic means to explicitly mention a single user via JID, but no means of mentioning via occupant ID, mentioning groups of users, nor means to extend mentions further.

This specification allows users to explicitly choose whether to mention a user or group, removing the need to use old regex-based name matches, which are prone to false positives. It also allows users to refer to someone else without disturbing them and without relying on tricks to prevent regex matches.
Mentions which contain an 'xml:lang' attribute correspond to body elements of the same language. Implementations SHOULD send a mention for every language it sends.

Setting MUC permissions can mitigate abuse of the ability to mention groups of people. This specification makes no attempt to mitigate abuse of implicit mentions; implementations SHOULD allow them to be turned off.
Use of explicit mentions with OMEMO could leak metadata about who the message is directed towards, if only the body of the message is encrypted.

Using &xep0421; instead of a regular participant JID in MUC prevents moderators possibly leaking JIDs they have access to that other occupants don't. Using occupant IDs even in non-anonymous rooms allows them to become semi-anonymous without leaking JIDs.
None.

section2.p: The XMPP Registrar shall add '&NS;' to its registry of protocol namespaces.

section2.code:
<form_type> <name>http://jabber.org/protocol/muc#roomconfig</name> <doc>XEP-0045</doc> <field var='mentions#count' type='text-single' label='How many mentions are allowed in a message?'/> <field var='mentions#individual' type='list-single' label='Who can mention individuals?'> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field>
<field var='mentions#channel' type='list-single' label='Who can mention room occupants?'> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field>
<field var='mentions#associations' type='list-single' label='Who can mention roles/affiliations?'> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field>
<field var='mentions#hats' type='list-single' label='Who can mention users with hats?'> <option label='Participants'> <value>participants</value> </option> <option label='Moderators Only'> <value>moderators</value> </option> <option label='Nobody'> <value>none</value> </option> </field> </form_type>

section1.code:
<?xml version='1.0' encoding='UTF-8'?> <xs:schema xmlns:xs='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema' targetNamespace='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' xmlns='urn:xmpp:mentions:0' elementFormDefault='qualified'>
<xs:annotation> <xs:documentation> The protocol documented by this schema is defined in XEP-xxxx: http://www.xmpp.org/extensions/inbox/explicit-mentions.html </xs:documentation> </xs:annotation>
<xs:element name='mention'> <xs:complexType> <xs:attribute name='active' type='xs:boolean'/> <xs:attribute name='type' type='xs:anyURI' use='required'/> <xs:attribute name='target' type='xs:string'/> <xs:attribute name='begin' type='xs:nonNegativeInteger'/> <xs:attribute name='end' type='xs:nonNegativeInteger'/> </xs:complexType> </xs:element>
</xs:schema>

section1.p: Special thanks to pep for providing the initial document which this one is based off of, and for allowing me to continue work on it.
